summary: Trump's sweeping global tariffs — a 10% baseline on all imports with steeper rates on dozens of countries — have rattled equity markets and stoked recession fears. Simultaneously, the Iran conflict is pressing the dollar lower, with markets watching for a breakthrough before Trump's deadline demanding uninterrupted Strait of Hormuz transit, while Axios reported US strikes on Kharg Island today.
tweet: Tariff shock and US strikes on Iran's Kharg Island are the twin catalysts today — dollar sliding, gold surging, bonds bid as traders seek cover.
